title: Pianist Transformer: Towards Expressive Piano Performance Rendering via Scalable Self-Supervised Pre-Training
abstract: arXiv:2512.02652v2 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Existing methods for expressive music performance rendering, a conditional generation task that aims to generate a human-like performance from a symbolic score, rely on supervised learning over small labeled datasets, which limits scaling of both data volume and model size, despite the availability of vast unlabeled music, as in vision and language. To address this gap, we introduce Pianist Transformer, with three key contributions: 1) introducing large-scale self-supervised learning into expressive piano performance rendering through a unified Musical Instrument Digital Interface (MIDI) representation, enabling pre-training on 10B tokens of unlabeled MIDI data; 2) an efficient asymmetric Transformer with note-level compression, substantially improving training efficiency, memory usage, and inference speed for long-context music modeling; 3) a state-of-the-art rendering model with an editable workflow, achieving strong objectiv

Title: Pianist Transformer: Towards Expressive Piano Performance Rendering via Scalable Self-Supervised Pre-Training
Abstract: arXiv:2512.02652v2 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Existing methods for expressive music performance rendering, a conditional generation task that aims to generate a human-like performance from a symbolic score, rely on supervised learning over small labeled datasets, which limits scaling of both data volume and model size, despite the availability of vast unlabeled music, as in vision and language. To address this gap, we introduce Pianist Transformer, with three key contributions: 1) introducing large-scale self-supervised learning into expressive piano performance rendering through a unified Musical Instrument Digital Interface (MIDI) representation, enabling pre-training on 10B tokens of unlabeled MIDI data; 2) an efficient asymmetric Transformer with note-level compression, substantially improving training efficiency, memory usage, and inference speed for long-context music modeling; 3) a state-of-the-art rendering model with an editable workflow, achieving strong objectiv
translated_title: 钢琴家Transformer：通过可扩展的自监督预训练实现富有表现力的钢琴演奏渲染
translated_abstract: arXiv:2512.02652v2 公告类型：替换交叉 摘要：现有用于富有表现力的音乐演奏渲染的方法（一种条件生成任务，旨在从符号乐谱生成类似人类的演奏）依赖于对小型标注数据集的监督学习，这限制了数据量和模型规模的扩展，尽管存在大量未标注的音乐数据（如在视觉和语言领域）。为解决这一差距，我们引入了钢琴家Transformer，其包含三个关键贡献：1）通过统一的乐器数字接口（MIDI）表示，将大规模自监督学习引入富有表现力的钢琴演奏渲染，使得能够在100亿个未标注MIDI数据标记上进行预训练；2）一种高效的带有音符级压缩的非对称Transformer，显著提高了长上下文音乐建模的训练效率、内存使用和推理速度；3）一个具有可编辑工作流程的最先进渲染模型，实现了强大的客观性能。
tldr: 钢琴家Transformer通过引入大规模自监督预训练、高效的非对称Transformer架构和可编辑工作流程，显著提升了钢琴演奏渲染的表现力和可扩展性。
en_tdlr: Pianist Transformer significantly enhances the expressiveness and scalability of piano performance rendering by introducing large-scale self-supervised pre-training, an efficient asymmetric Transformer architecture, and an editable workflow.
